title: [SRE] operational excellence â€” alert-runbook coverage, alert quality, error budgets, operational incidents, dependency health
scope: Tier-5 operations + GTM, cycle 2 of 3 (per project_propmanager_path_forward.md). Phase 31 [ONBOARDING] closed 18/18; Phase 32 [SRE] is the operational-maturity cycle that turns the existing alert + SLO surface into a runnable on-call program. Today's surface: 29 runbooks under docs/runbooks/ (workflow-automation, integrations, onboarding, etc.) but NO machine-readable mapping of alert -> runbook; an alert fires, the on-call person has to grep docs/. config/observability.php defines slo.evaluation_window_days + per-route-class p95 budgets; slo:report aggregates http_request_ms but there is NO error-budget burn rate calculation, NO budget remaining gauge, NO multi-window burn-rate alert pattern. SecurityIncident model covers ATTACKS (failed logins, webhook signature floods, large exports) but there is NO operational incident model for non-security outages (DB slowdown, queue wedge, third-party Daraja outage). MetricsService emits ~30 gauges via Phase-22 + Phase-29 + Phase-30 + Phase-31 cadence but nothing tracks WHICH gauges actually trip thresholds (alert_firings table missing) â€” we cannot compute signal-to-noise per alert to identify the noisy ones that contribute to fatigue. failed-jobs-growth-monitor (Phase-5 OBS-13) pages on email-blast but there is NO structured alert registry to centralise on-call routing, severity, runbook link. Phase-13 BREACH-7 mentions 'post-incident review template' but no actual blameless-postmortem document exists. DependencyHealthService doesn't exist â€” no machine-checked status for Daraja/Paystack/IntaSend/SMTP/SMS/Redis upstream dependencies; if Daraja is down the only signal is failed M-Pesa transactions. No Phase32SreSurfaceTest watchdog. No lang/{en,sw}/sre.php (would Phase24CiTest reject the new keys). No docs/runbooks/sre.md (the runbook ABOUT runbooks). 18 findings across 6 categories: SRE-RUNBOOK (3 â€” alerts.yaml registry as single source of truth, RunbookCoverageAudit cron flagging alerts without a linked runbook, runbook-staleness audit comparing last-edited vs production fire frequency), SRE-ALERT (3 â€” alert_firings table + AlertFiringRecorder + alert:quality cron emitting alert_signal_to_noise_ratio gauges and alert_fatigue_count), SRE-BUDGET (3 â€” service_slos table per-tier, ErrorBudgetCalculator over rolling 30-day window, slo:budget-audit cron emitting service_slo_budget_remaining_pct + multi-window burn-rate gauges), SRE-INCIDENT (3 â€” operational_incidents table separate from SecurityIncident, blameless post-mortem markdown template + OpsIncidentController CRUD, mttr:audit cron emitting MTTR p50/p90 by severity), SRE-DEPS (3 â€” DependencyHealthService periodic poll of Daraja/Paystack/Redis/SMTP/SMS + dependency_health_status enum + outbound:health cron emitting dependency_up gauge + DegradationDetected event), SRE-CI (3 â€” Phase32SreSurfaceTest watchdog, lang/{en,sw}/sre.php namespace, docs/runbooks/sre.md). Severity 5H/8M/5L. Builds on Phase-13 SecurityIncident, Phase-14 OBSERV alert-thresholds.md, Phase-22 PERF-SLO + slo:report + MetricsService::gauge continuous-time-series, Phase-29 workflow:health silent-failure pattern, Phase-30 audit pattern, Phase-31 milestone-recorder idempotent-write pattern.

findings.title: config/alerts.php registry â€” single source of truth for alerts
findings.evidence: Today, alert metadata (name, severity, threshold, runbook URL) is spread across docs/runbooks/alert-thresholds.md (markdown table), config/observability.php (per-route budgets), and inline values in IncidentDetector + various artisan commands. Nobody can answer 'what is the canonical list of alerts and which runbook does each map to' without reading prose. The on-call person hit by 'failed_jobs growth alert' shouldn't have to grep code to find the runbook section.
findings.fix: (a) config/alerts.php returns array of alert definitions: ['key' => 'failed_jobs_growth', 'severity' => 'high', 'threshold' => 25, 'window' => '24h', 'gauge' => 'failed_jobs_count', 'runbook' => 'docs/runbooks/queue-triage.md#failed-jobs-growth', 'paging' => 'email|page', 'description' => '...']; (b) AlertRegistry::all() service returns the parsed array; (c) inventory the 20-ish alerts currently scattered.

findings.title: RunbookCoverageAudit cron â€” flag alerts with broken runbook links
findings.evidence: An alert with a runbook link pointing to a missing markdown anchor is silently broken â€” the on-call person clicks, gets a 404 or the wrong section, and improvises. Without an audit nobody notices until they're paged at 3am.
findings.fix: (a) artisan runbook:coverage-audit walks AlertRegistry::all() and for each entry verifies the runbook file exists AND the anchor (everything after #) matches a heading in the markdown; (b) emits runbook_coverage_broken_links_count gauge; (c) prints a table of broken refs to stdout for CI mode; (d) Schedule weeklyOn Sunday 06:00 Africa/Nairobi.

findings.title: Runbook staleness gauge â€” runbooks not edited in 180 days that back active alerts
findings.evidence: A runbook that hasn't been touched in 180 days while its alert fires monthly is suspicious â€” either the runbook is wrong or the alert is. Either way an operator should look.
findings.fix: (a) artisan runbook:staleness-audit walks AlertRegistry, stat()s each runbook file, computes days since modification; emits runbook_staleness_days{runbook=X} gauge AND runbook_stale_count (count where days > 180); (b) Schedule weeklyOn Sunday 06:30.

findings.title: alert_firings table + AlertFiringRecorder service
findings.evidence: Currently a gauge crossing a threshold dispatches an alert (email or queue check) but there is no PERSISTED log of which alerts fired, when, with what value. Cannot compute alert-fatigue metrics, cannot answer 'how often does the failed_jobs alert fire false-positive vs true-positive', cannot do post-mortem retrospectives on alert behaviour. The log is the foundation for SRE-ALERT-2 (signal-to-noise) and SRE-BUDGET-3 (multi-window burn rate alerts).
findings.fix: (a) Migration alert_firings(alert_key, severity, value, threshold, fired_at, resolved_at, acknowledged_by, acknowledgement_note, metadata json); (b) Model with no TenantScope (system-wide); (c) AlertFiringRecorder::record(alertKey, value, threshold, metadata) + ::resolve(alertKey) + ::acknowledge(alertKey, userId, note); (d) refactor IncidentDetector + failed-jobs-growth-monitor to call the recorder.

findings.title: alert:quality cron emitting signal-to-noise ratio
findings.evidence: Once alert_firings exists we can score each alert's quality: signal_count = fires resolved by operator action; noise_count = fires auto-resolved within 5min (probably transient blip); ratio = signal / (signal+noise). A ratio below 0.5 is alert fatigue territory and the threshold should be retuned. Without this, on-call people learn to ignore the noisy alerts â€” exactly the muscle memory you don't want.
findings.fix: (a) artisan alert:quality emits alert_signal_to_noise_ratio{alert_key=X} for each alert over the last 30 days, AND alert_fatigue_count (count of alerts where ratio < 0.5); (b) Schedule dailyAt 06:00 Africa/Nairobi onOneServer.

findings.title: AlertFiringRecorded event + workflow_runs_log audit trail
findings.evidence: Alert fires should flow into the same workflow_runs_log pipe as Phase-29 workflows + Phase-31 milestones so workflow:health surfaces alert volume alongside cron health.
findings.fix: (a) AlertFiringRecorded event fired by recorder; (b) LogAlertFiring listener auto-discovered, writes WorkflowLogger row workflowName='alert:fired' action=alert_key.

findings.title: service_slos table + ErrorBudgetCalculator
findings.evidence: slo.md documents target uptime per tier (99.5%, 99.9%, etc.) but those numbers live in markdown only â€” not in the database, not enforced, not measured. ErrorBudgetCalculator needs a typed source: per-service window + objective + good/bad indicator queries. Without first-class storage, budget calculations are fragile to refactors.
findings.fix: (a) Migration service_slos(service_key, tier, window_days, objective_pct, good_indicator_metric, bad_indicator_metric, is_active); (b) Model (no TenantScope, system-wide); (c) ErrorBudgetCalculator::compute(serviceKey) returns ['target_pct', 'achieved_pct', 'budget_total_minutes', 'budget_consumed_minutes', 'budget_remaining_pct', 'burn_rate_1h', 'burn_rate_6h']; (d) seed the 4 tiers from slo.md as the initial rows.

findings.title: slo:budget-audit cron emitting budget gauges
findings.evidence: ErrorBudgetCalculator is internal â€” the operator-visible signal is a Prometheus gauge. Without continuous emission the budget remaining is unknown until someone runs slo:report manually.
findings.fix: (a) artisan slo:budget-audit walks active ServiceSlos, calls ErrorBudgetCalculator::compute, emits service_slo_budget_remaining_pct{service=X} + service_slo_burn_rate_1h{service=X} + service_slo_burn_rate_6h{service=X} gauges; (b) Schedule everyFifteenMinutes Africa/Nairobi onOneServer (matches Phase-22 PERF-SLO cadence).

findings.title: Multi-window burn-rate alert wiring
findings.evidence: Google's SRE Workbook multi-window burn rate (1h fast, 6h slow) is the industry standard for SLO alerting â€” fires only when budget is being consumed fast AND sustained. Single-window (1h) alone produces too many false positives. The Phase-32 path stitches the burn-rate calculator (SRE-BUDGET-1) to the alert recorder (SRE-ALERT-1).
findings.fix: (a) SloBudgetAudit cron computes 1h + 6h burn rates per service; (b) if 1h burn > 14.4x AND 6h burn > 6x for any service, fires AlertFiringRecorder->record('slo_budget_fast_burn', value, threshold, metadata=[service, ratio_1h, ratio_6h]); (c) entry in config/alerts.php registry.

findings.title: operational_incidents table â€” non-security operational events
findings.evidence: SecurityIncident (Phase-13) covers attacks (failed logins, webhook signature floods, large exports). It does NOT cover OPERATIONAL incidents: queue wedge, DB slowdown, Daraja outage, dependency degradation. Today an op-incident lives only in the slack message + the operator's memory. Without first-class storage, MTTR cannot be measured, post-mortems cannot be linked, repeat incidents cannot be detected.
findings.fix: (a) Migration operational_incidents(title, severity enum [sev1, sev2, sev3, sev4], status enum [open, investigating, mitigated, resolved], opened_at, mitigated_at, resolved_at, opened_by_user_id, resolved_by_user_id, affected_services json, summary, root_cause, post_mortem_url); (b) Model (system-wide, no TenantScope); (c) status machine guard methods (canMitigate, canResolve).

findings.title: Blameless post-mortem template + OpsIncidentController CRUD
findings.evidence: Phase-13 BREACH-7 mentions a 30-day review for SecurityIncidents but there is no actual template document landlords/ops can use. Industry-standard blameless post-mortem (summary, timeline, root cause, contributing factors, customer impact, action items with owners + due dates) is the artefact every Tier-5 SaaS produces. Need both the template AND the controller to record the URL + status against an OperationalIncident.
findings.fix: (a) post-mortem-template.md with the 7 standard sections; (b) OpsIncidentController::index|store|show|update|setPostMortem â€” admin-only middleware; (c) routes under /ops/incidents/*.

findings.title: mttr:audit cron emitting MTTR p50/p90 by severity
findings.evidence: MTTR (mean time to resolve) is the canonical SRE leadership metric. Without it leadership cannot see whether incident response is getting better or worse over quarters.
findings.fix: (a) artisan mttr:audit walks operational_incidents resolved in last 90 days; computes mttr_minutes per severity; emits operational_incident_mttr_p50_minutes{severity=X} + operational_incident_mttr_p90_minutes{severity=X} gauges; (b) Schedule weeklyOn Monday 06:45 Africa/Nairobi.

findings.title: DependencyHealthService â€” periodic upstream poll
findings.evidence: If Daraja goes down, our only signal is failed M-Pesa transactions piling up â€” minutes-to-hours later. A dedicated health-check service that pings each upstream every 5 minutes catches degradations BEFORE they show as customer impact. Industry pattern: every SaaS at scale has a 'status page checker' for its dependencies.
findings.fix: (a) DependencyHealthService::check(depKey) returns ['status' => 'up|degraded|down', 'latency_ms' => N, 'checked_at' => now]; supports 'daraja', 'paystack', 'intasend', 'smtp' (via Mail::raw to a test address blackholed), 'sms', 'redis' (ping); (b) per-dep timeout = 5s, no retry (the check itself should not hide degradation); (c) idempotent via short Cache::add (60s) so multiple invocations within a minute coalesce.

findings.title: outbound:health cron + dependency_up gauges
findings.evidence: Service is internal â€” need a cron that walks the dep list every 5 minutes and emits a Prometheus gauge so Grafana can alert on outages.
findings.fix: (a) artisan outbound:health-check walks configured dependencies; emits dependency_up{dep=X} (1 = up, 0.5 = degraded, 0 = down) + dependency_latency_ms{dep=X}; (b) Schedule everyFiveMinutes Africa/Nairobi onOneServer; (c) on transition from up->down, fires DegradationDetected event.

findings.title: DegradationDetected event + LogDegradationDetected listener
findings.evidence: Degradation events must flow into the unified workflow_runs_log so workflow:health and the operator dashboard surface them alongside other operational events.
findings.fix: (a) DegradationDetected dispatched from OutboundHealthCheck on transition; (b) LogDegradationDetected listener auto-discovered, writes WorkflowLogger row workflowName='dependency:degradation' action='up_to_down|down_to_up', metadata with previous + current status.

findings.title: Phase32SreSurfaceTest watchdog
findings.evidence: Phase 29-31 established the surface-watchdog pattern. Phase 32 ships 6 crons (runbook:coverage-audit, runbook:staleness-audit, alert:quality, slo:budget-audit, mttr:audit, outbound:health-check) + 2 events (AlertFiringRecorded, DegradationDetected) + 1 i18n namespace (sre). Without the watchdog, a future refactor can silently drop a cron.
findings.fix: (a) test_every_phase32_scheduler_is_registered_with_correct_cadence: EXPECTED_SCHEDULES asserts all 6 crons with cron expression + Africa/Nairobi; (b) test_every_phase32_event_has_a_listener: EXPECTED_EVENTS = [AlertFiringRecorded, DegradationDetected]; (c) test_phase32_sre_lang_namespace_exists_with_parity asserts lang/{en,sw}/sre.php loadable + key sets identical.

findings.title: lang/{en,sw}/sre.php namespace + Phase24CiTest parity
findings.evidence: Every new user-facing string (incident creation UI labels, post-mortem template headers, alert acknowledgement form) must have an English + Swahili pair. Phase24CiTest enforces key-set identity (ORDER must match); the sw file is built mirror-of-en upfront.
findings.fix: Mirror sections for: incident (status_open, status_investigating, status_mitigated, status_resolved, severity_sev1, severity_sev2, severity_sev3, severity_sev4), alert (acknowledge_button, acknowledged_success), post_mortem (heading, sections.summary, sections.timeline, sections.root_cause, sections.contributing_factors, sections.customer_impact, sections.action_items, sections.lessons_learned), dependency (status_up, status_degraded, status_down).

findings.title: docs/runbooks/sre.md operator reference
findings.evidence: Phase 28-31 established the runbook cadence. Phase 32 IS the SRE phase â€” it needs its own runbook that lists the alert registry behaviour, the error-budget burn-rate decision tree, when to declare an OperationalIncident vs a SecurityIncident, how to file a blameless post-mortem, dependency-health interpretation.
findings.fix: Sections: Overview (Phase 32 surface map), Alert registry (config/alerts.php contract + how to add an alert), Error budgets (how the burn-rate calculation works, multi-window rationale, when to halt deploys), Operational incidents (sev1-4 definitions, declaring vs resolving, post-mortem cadence), Dependency health (5-minute poll cadence, upstream-by-upstream remediation), CI gates (Phase32SreSurfaceTest + alert:quality + slo:budget-audit + runbook:coverage-audit), Deferrals.
